creator: vbasiccd@msn.com
text:
I discovered the problem.  One of the dependencies is "textproc/gnugrep", which has a "PCRE" config option. By default this is enabled, but in my Poudriere repository make.conf, I unset PCRE in favor of PCRE2. This of course unset PCRE in the gnugrep port, which in turn caused the "disable-perl-regexp build" error while building dotnet. I reset PCRE in the gnugrep port and the Poudriere build completed successfully.

I did notice in the logs that gnugrep used PCRE2, so maybe the config option should be renamed to "PCRE2"? Because it's currently named "PCRE" is why it was automatically unset by my repository make.conf.
